sourceLabel: Aarohan JEE Academy courses and batches

entries.title: Class 11 two-year JEE batch
entries.content: For Class 11 students, Aarohan runs a two-year JEE foundation and advanced preparation track. The usual structure includes 4 to 5 classroom teaching days each week, weekly problem-practice sheets, periodic tests, and a weekend doubt window. This batch is meant for students starting serious JEE preparation from Class 11.

entries.title: Class 12 target batch
entries.content: For Class 12 students, Aarohan offers a one-year target batch that balances board exam pressure with JEE Main and Advanced preparation. The plan typically combines weekday subject classes, chapter tests, board-aligned revision blocks, and focused problem-solving for Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics.

entries.title: Dropper rank-improvement batch
entries.content: Dropper batches are designed for students taking a repeat attempt after Class 12. These batches are usually more intensive, with faster syllabus coverage, scheduled test analysis, rank-improvement planning, and mentor check-ins for consistency. The counsellor generally confirms current seat availability before promising a specific dropper batch.

entries.title: Hybrid and online academic support
entries.content: Aarohan is not a fully recorded self-paced platform. Classroom students receive hybrid support such as live doubt sessions, revision handouts, attendance follow-up, and limited access to online recap material when a class is missed. The admissions counsellor confirms the exact mix of classroom and online support for the selected batch.
